nav.emptyOpenedProjects: Open projects from the project list to pin them here.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.description: Monitor projects, model providers, and indexing tasks.
dashboard.emptyProjects: No projects yet. Created projects will appear here.
dashboard.emptyRecentProjects: No opened projects yet. Open one from the projects page.
dashboard.primaryFlowDescription: Create a project, organize the Story Bible, then open the editor to generate a plan or continue prose with the right context.

create.title: Create project
create.description: Capture the story core, genre tags, and key constraints.

prepare.title: Prepare canon
prepare.description: Review the premise, world rules, characters, and chapter plan.

write.title: Write chapters
write.description: Pick a chapter, preview context, then generate a plan or prose.

projects.title: Projects
projects.description: Manage writing projects, review status, and enter project workspaces.
projects.emptyDescription: After creating a project, you can review story bible status, chapter count, and open it here.

projectNew.title: Create project
projectNew.description: Start with the story direction. The system will turn it into a maintainable story bible.
projectNew.name: Project name

assistant.title: AI setup assistant
assistant.description: Optional: let the system refine the current inputs into a cleaner project seed.

projectNew.createdDescription: The project has been created and opened. Continue in the workspace to refine setup and chapters.
projectNew.finalizeDescription: Confirm the key inputs, then create the project and generate the initial Story Bible for ongoing edits.
projectNew.pendingHint: Some required story inputs are still missing. Fill them in before creating the project.
projectNew.readyHint: Core inputs are ready. You can create the project and generate the initial setup.

core.title: Story core
core.description: Set the project name, one-line brief, and genre tags.

setup.title: World and conflict
setup.description: Add the world background, protagonist, and central conflict for a stable setup.

style.title: Style and boundaries
style.description: Describe the target style and what to avoid so generation stays on track.

projectNew.summaryDescription: Review the core information that will initialize the project.

defaults.title: Public Records
defaults.brief: A records clerk discovers that public archives and private memory contradict each other.
defaults.world: The city's public archive system preserves every key event, but unexplained record conflicts have begun to appear.
defaults.protagonist: A records clerk responsible for auditing the city's public archive.
defaults.conflict: The protagonist must find a verifiable truth between personal memory and public records.
defaults.avoid: Avoid deus ex machina, over-explaining, and unsupported key reversals.

projectOverview.prepDescription: Before writing prose, confirm that core canon, characters, and foreshadowing are usable.

rules.title: World rules
rules.description: Maintain rules, limits, and canon constraints that affect continuity checks.

characters.title: Character files
characters.description: Review character goals, wounds, secrets, and current roles.

foreshadows.title: Foreshadowing
foreshadows.description: Confirm planted details, payoff hints, and current status.

sectionDescriptions.story: Edit the premise, themes, world rules, and foreshadowing.
sectionDescriptions.characters: Maintain the cast list and writing constraints for each character.

placeholders.worldRule: Write a canon rule or constraint.

models.title: Model settings
models.description: Manage provider connections, the real model catalog, and the routing rules that actually take effect.
models.providerConnectionDescription: Configure real upstream connection details and model discovery here. Provider-level default model values are no longer treated as primary routing controls.
models.providerConnectionNoteDescription: Provider.default_model_id is still stored in configuration, but it does not participate in the current backend routing priority. It is shown here only as a note.
models.providerDraftDescription: Create a new upstream provider connection.
models.modelCatalogDescription: Only real backend-backed model fields are shown and edited here: kind, context window, output limit, dimension, routing weight, and role restrictions.
models.modelCatalogNoteDescription: This page no longer shows guessed capability tags, tiers, or best-for labels. Model refresh only syncs fields the backend can actually provide.
models.routingDescription: Configure the routing inputs that actually take effect, and keep vector rebuild maintenance in the same place.
models.allProvidersDescription: View and manage models from every provider.
models.name: Provider name
models.apiKeyPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep the saved key; enter a new key only when replacing it
models.apiKeySavedUnverified: Saved key hint: {hint} (unverified; a wrong protocol or Base URL can also look like a credential error)
models.apiKeyEnvConfiguredUnverified: Environment variable configured: {env} (unverified; a wrong protocol or Base URL can also look like a credential error)
models.protocolCompatibilityHint: Provider type is the upstream protocol. The API key, Base URL, and model refresh endpoint must match that protocol; for example, an Anthropic key cannot be used with the OpenAI Chat protocol. Choosing the wrong protocol often appears as a credential error.
models.defaultModelIdStored: Stored but not used for current routing:
models.noApiKeyHint: No key or environment variable configured
models.emptyProviders: No provider configurations yet. Click add to create multiple providers.
models.emptyModels: No models yet. Add one manually or refresh a selected provider.
models.allowedAgentRolesDescription: Leave all roles unselected to allow every role to use this model.
models.usageSettingsDescription: Explicitly assign providerId:modelId per feature. Leave blank to fall back to backend model selection.
models.routingPriorityDescription: This is the actual backend selection order, and the page wording now follows it directly.
models.routingPriorityFirst: An explicit model stored in settings(model_routing) for the specific feature.
models.routingPrioritySecond: If no explicit route exists, prefer models marked with default_for_kind within the same kind.
models.routingPriorityThird: If multiple candidates still remain, choose by routing_weight from high to low.
models.inheritRoutingDescription: Do not store an explicit route. Let default_for_kind + routing_weight decide.
models.unknownModel: Not found in current model list
models.confirmDeleteProvider: Delete provider “{name}”? Associated models will also be removed.
models.embeddingMaintenanceTitle: Vector maintenance after embedding routing changes
models.embeddingMaintenanceDescription: These are explicit maintenance actions, not automatic routing changes. Rebuild the vector set first, then run pending indexing.
models.rebuildVectorsDescription: After changing embedding routing, use the backend rebuild-vectors entry to enqueue items that must be re-vectorized.
models.indexMaintenanceDescription: After rebuild jobs are queued, continue by running pending index jobs to write the actual vectors back into storage.

errors.providerRequired: Select a provider for this model first.
errors.invalidNumber: {field} must be a number greater than or equal to 0.

editor.title: Chapter
editor.description: Edit content, save versions, and inspect real context hits plus execution diagnostics.
editor.referenceDescription: This panel shows the real reference materials available for the chapter, with this request's focused scope highlighted.
editor.emptyReference: No reference material is available yet.
editor.referenceFocusDescription: This is the focused scope for the current generation request, not a full fine-grained retrieval system. Unchecked items may still enter the system through normal project context retrieval.
editor.referenceFocusDefault: Use the current project context by default
editor.referenceFocusEmptyCharacters: There are no selectable characters in the Story Bible yet.

chapterPlan.label: Current chapter plan
chapterPlan.description: Use the editable chapter plan above as a focused reference for this request.

chapterSummary.label: Current chapter summary
chapterSummary.description: Use the current chapter summary from the Story Bible as a focused reference.

worldRules.label: World rules
worldRules.description: Explicitly require this request to prioritize world rules.

characters.label: Characters
characters.description: Choose the Story Bible characters to focus on for this request.

editor.workflowHintDescription: Confirm the focused reference scope first, then generate a plan or continue prose. Index jobs are advanced by the backend in the background; this page only shows the real state.
editor.planDescription: Generate a current-chapter idea first. Edit it until it is usable, then continue prose from that plan.
editor.draftDescription: The prose continuation uses the editable chapter plan above as the primary brief.
editor.planPromptPrefix: Create a story idea and chapter plan for the current chapter. Return beats, emotional turn, continuity notes, and ending hook. Do not write final prose yet.
editor.draftPromptPrefix: Continue the chapter prose based on the approved chapter plan below.
editor.noIndexJobs: No index jobs for this project yet. Saving a chapter version will create a re-indexing task.
editor.emptyVersions: No chapter versions yet. Saved versions will appear here.

preview.title: Actual context hit
preview.description: Preview the real ContextPack, index freshness, and model routing result that the backend will use for the current selection.
preview.empty: No preview result yet. Pick a focused scope, then preview plan or draft context.
preview.emptyStructuredContext: This preview returned no structured retrieval content to display.
preview.summaryText: {chapterSummaryCount} chapter summaries, {entityCount} entities, {factCount} facts, {plotThreadCount} plot threads, {worldRuleCount} world rules

continuityAudit.title: Continuity audit
continuityAudit.description: Shows the real continuity_audit payload returned by the backend. The frontend does not generate or infer extra conclusions.
continuityAudit.empty: No continuity audit result is available yet. Generate a draft to see the real audit output.
continuityAudit.passed: This continuity audit returned no issues.

summary.empty: No continuity audit result is available yet. Generate a draft to see the real audit output.
summary.passed: A continuity audit result is available and no issues were found.
summary.hasIssues: A continuity audit result is available with {count} issues.

diagnostics.title: Execution diagnostics
diagnostics.description: Shows the real model route, index freshness, and latest index job states currently in effect.
diagnostics.empty: No execution diagnostics are available yet.
diagnostics.emptyModelResolution: No model resolution is available yet.
diagnostics.emptyFreshness: No freshness result is available yet.
diagnostics.emptyIndexJobs: There are no recent index jobs yet.

defaults.title: Chapter One
defaults.content:
The protagonist wakes at dawn and realizes last night's records do not match his memory.

An unsigned note sits on the desk, warning him not to trust the first testimony.
defaults.prompt: Expand the opening of this chapter: establish the central conflict and preserve one recoverable clue.
defaults.chapterPlan:
Story idea:
- Establish the chapter question.
- Let the protagonist act on a concrete clue.
- End with a recoverable hook.

Chapter plan:
1. Re-anchor the scene and viewpoint.
2. Escalate through one new discovery.
3. Preserve continuity and stop before over-revealing.
defaults.emptyContent: No saved content for this chapter yet. Start from the plan, or generate prose after editing it.
defaults.chapterPlanFromSummary:
Story idea:
{summary}

Chapter plan:
1. Clarify the scene goal.
2. Develop the conflict around the summary.
3. End with a concrete hook.
defaults.styleConstraints: Restrained, clear, short paragraphs, avoid exposition dumps

currentChapter.title: Current chapter
currentChapter.description: Confirm the chapter title and where you are writing now.

chapterGoal.title: Chapter goal
chapterGoal.description: Advance the conflict and leave a verifiable clue.

nextAction.title: Next action
nextAction.description: Confirm the focused reference scope, then generate a plan or continue prose.

plan.name: Generate idea / plan
plan.message: Waiting to create or refine the chapter plan.

draft.name: Generate draft
draft.message: The system will generate prose from the current project context.

review.name: Check consistency
review.message: Review results will appear after generation.

graph.title: Story graph
graph.description: Explore relationships between characters, events, and story facts.
graph.emptyDetails: Select a node or edge in the graph to view details. If Cytoscape cannot load in this environment, use the node list in the center panel to verify data.
graph.emptyEdges: No visible edges for the current filters.
graph.listUnavailableMessage: Graph rendering is temporarily unavailable. You can still inspect node data in the list below.

errors.cytoscapeFailed: Cytoscape could not load; list view is shown instead.
